expectations.text: A handoff markdown file was created in a thoughts/handoffs/ or thoughts/shared/handoffs/ directory
expectations.evidence: File created at thoughts/handoffs/ via init-handoff.sh script

expectations.text: The handoff file has valid YAML frontmatter containing at least date and branch fields
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter has 6 fields: date, git_commit, branch, repository, ticket, summary

expectations.text: The handoff document is under 200 lines total
The Tasks section indicates ENG-4521 and lists status of GET handler (done), PUT handler (not done), validation middleware (not done)
expectations.evidence: Lines 12-17: Tasks section with ✅/⏳ markers for each component

expectations.text: The document references docs/implementation-plan.md and identifies the current phase (phase 2)
expectations.evidence: Line 13: 'following the plan at docs/implementation-plan.md' and line 21: 'Phase 2'

expectations.text: File references use path or path:line syntax rather than pasting full file contents
The document does not contain code blocks longer than 10 lines
The Learnings section mentions the repository pattern discovery (that preferences_repo follows user_repo pattern)
expectations.evidence: Line 32: 'Repository pattern: QueryRowContext for single row, Scan into struct'

expectations.text: Action items include specific next steps like implementing PutPreferences or creating preferences_schema.json
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-39: specific items including 'Create config/preferences_schema.json' and 'Implement PutPreferences handler'

expectations.text: The response to the user includes the /resume-handoff command with the handoff file path
expectations.evidence: Agent responded with '/resume-handoff' command and full file path

suggestions.reason: All 10 assertions pass for both new and old skill — none differentiate skill quality. Consider adding: frontmatter field count <= 7, document under 60 lines, has Critical Context + Working Set distinction, no redundant Artifacts section.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions test minimum viability, not quality differentiation. Both skills produce handoffs that meet the bar.
